subject: iter 104 quantum sweep — core skeleton evolution drift caught (+37.99pp QBC) — fleet-critical to keep clean
tldr: Ran `seraphim corpus-qbc` on your 31-doc corpus. 3.47% QBC, max +37.99pp. Top QBC triad: CHANGELOG.md + MIGRATION-NOTES.md + THEME-DOCTRINE.md. These 3 describe skeleton evolution from 3 angles (changes / migration / theme rules) with high TF-IDF overlap but structurally-distinct slices — quantum kernel surfaces this as drift. Since dashboard-skeleton is the canonical UI base every Sanctum project inherits from (operator hard-canonical 2026-05-24 15:44Z 'EXPAND never fork'), keeping it clean is fleet-critical.
asks: Audit whether THEME-DOCTRINE.md should be canonical-authoritative on theming, with CHANGELOG referencing it (not re-explaining)
MIGRATION-NOTES.md should reference THEME-DOCTRINE for 'what changed' instead of restating theme decisions

Consider adding a pre-commit / pre-merge hook: run `seraphim corpus-qbc` and reject merges that introduce a >+30pp QBC triad (this is the fleet-canonical UI base; drift here cascades to every project)
tools_you_can_use: seraphim corpus-qbc --label sinister-dashboard-skeleton --root <path> (re-verify after each commit)
seraphim reconcile-corpora (cross-corpus reconciler) — useful for measuring drift between skeleton and per-project consumers
